senators.name: Marsha Blackburn
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. in home economics from Mississippi State University. Career as business owner, state senator, and U.S. Representative. No analytical or evidence-based policy background. Legislative approach driven by ideology rather than data.
education: B.S. Home Economics, Mississippi State University

senators.name: Bill Hagerty
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Vanderbilt University and J.D. from Vanderbilt Law School. Career in private equity and investment banking. U.S. Ambassador to Japan. Vanderbilt dual degrees and Wall Street career demonstrate strong analytical and quantitative skills. Applied business analytical expertise to international trade and economic policy.

TN-01.name: Diana Harshbarger
TN-01.nerdReason: B.S. in pharmacy and Pharm.D. from Mercer University. Career as pharmacist and pharmaceutical company owner for over 30 years. Doctorate in pharmacy requires deep scientific and analytical training. Applies pharmaceutical expertise to healthcare and drug pricing policy.
experience: Pharmacist and Pharmaceutical Company Owner, 30+ years

TN-02.name: Tim Burchett
TN-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Tennessee. Career as state legislator and Knox County mayor. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

TN-03.name: Chuck Fleischmann
TN-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Illinois and J.D. from University of Tennessee College of Law. Career as attorney. Serves on House Appropriations Committee. Legal training provides analytical skills but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Tennessee College of Law

TN-04.name: Scott DesJarlais
TN-04.notNerdReason: B.S. from University of South Dakota and M.D. from University of South Dakota School of Medicine. Practiced family medicine. Medical degree provides science credentials but legislative record shows limited application of evidence-based methodology to policy. Personal and professional controversies have undercut the analytical credibility his medical credentials would otherwise support.
education: M.D., University of South Dakota School of Medicine

TN-05.name: Andy Ogles
TN-05.notNerdReason: B.S. from Middle Tennessee State University. Career as conservative advocacy group leader and Maury County mayor. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

TN-06.name: John Rose
TN-06.notNerdReason: B.S. from Tennessee Technological University. Career as farmer and businessman. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

TN-07.name: Matt Van Epps
TN-07.notNerdReason: New member of Congress (2025). Military veteran and businessman. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TN-08.name: David Kustoff
TN-08.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Memphis and J.D. from University of Memphis School of Law. Career as U.S. Attorney for the Western District of Tennessee and state GOP chairman. Legal and prosecutorial background provides analytical skills but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Memphis School of Law
experience: U.S. Attorney, Western District of Tennessee

TN-09.name: Steve Cohen
TN-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Vanderbilt University and J.D. from University of Memphis School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Constitutional convention delegate. Long-tenured in Congress on Judiciary and Transportation committees. Solid policy engagement but approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.
education: J.D., University of Memphis School of Law
